$comment: Tier-2 DB-state fixture spec for the `doc_mount_chunks` repo — a MOUNT-INDEX
sibling-DB BLOB repo of Phase 2 (a Float32 embedding BLOB, two REAL-int
columns, a nullable TEXT column, UUID-as-TEXT refs). One committed file drives
BOTH sides of the differential:
- the v4 oracle (build-doc-mount-chunks-fixture.ts + doc-mount-chunks-tier2.ts)
- the Rust port (quilltap-harness doc_mount_chunks tier-2 test).
SIBLING DB: in v4 this table lives in quilltap-mount-index.db (NOT the main
DB). The builder/oracle point SQLITE_MOUNT_INDEX_PATH at the fixture and read
it back through getRawMountIndexDatabase(); the Rust Writer simply opens that
file by path. Mirrors the doc_mount_points / group_character_members machinery
exactly. The repo's overridden getCollection() creates the table (lazy CREATE
TABLE IF NOT EXISTS) + two indexes on first access — no explicit
ensureCollection needed. The indexes don't affect column layout or row bytes.
BLOB column (after `help_docs` / `conversation_chunks`): `embedding` stores a
raw Float32 byte buffer (little-endian f32 bytes). v4's `documentToRow`
converts a number[]/Float32Array to a BLOB via `embeddingToBlob`; an EMPTY
array or null is stored as NULL. The canonical dump emits BLOBs as lowercase
hex on BOTH sides, so deterministic embeddings compare bit-exact.
EMBEDDING VALUES are chosen to be EXACTLY float32-representable so the
f64->f32 cast is lossless and identical on both sides. [0.5,-0.25,0.75,0.125]
encodes little-endian as `0000003f000080be0000403f0000003e`.
AFFINITY: chunkIndex/tokenCount are z.number().int().min(0) (min only, no max)
-> REAL by v4's mapToSQLiteType (bound f64). The canonical dump collapses
integer-valued REAL back to a JSON integer, so 0/5/etc. match v4.
BANKED BEHAVIOR: a text-only-ish `update` (content + tokenCount + heading) on
a row that HAS an embedding must LEAVE the embedding BLOB untouched. v4's
`_update` rewrites the whole row from the hydrated existing entity (the BLOB
round-trips losslessly); the Rust partial `UPDATE SET` simply never names the
embedding column. The post-update dump must still show the original embedding
hex — op #3 below exercises this. (v4's updateEmbedding, which DOES mutate the
BLOB, is out of scope.)
SAFETY: testPepperBase64 is a THROWAWAY 32-byte key, the same one every other
tier-2 fixture uses. It is NOT the real Friday pepper and is safe to commit.
DETERMINISM: every id and timestamp is pinned, so both sides produce
byte-identical post-op state and the canonical dump needs ZERO normalization.
BANKED across the corpus: embedding present (hex) and null; headingContext
both non-null and null; REAL-int chunkIndex/tokenCount both 0 and >0; the
BLOB-survives-text-update invariant.

seed.content: The Salon convenes at the stroke of midnight.
Prospero unfurls a map of the airship routes.

ops.comment: New chunk WITH an embedding + a heading. Exercises the BLOB insert path (number[] -> little-endian Float32 bytes -> hex `0000003f000080be0000403f0000003e`) and a non-null headingContext. chunkIndex 2, tokenCount > 0. id + timestamps pinned.

data.content: Pascal tallies the ledger of the night's invitations.

ops.comment: New chunk with NULL embedding + NULL heading. Exercises the null -> SQL NULL embedding path and a null headingContext. chunkIndex 3, tokenCount 0.

data.content: Carina charts the constellations above the dirigible.

ops.comment: Update on the EMBEDDED seed row (chunk c1): change content + tokenCount + headingContext only (NO embedding field). The embedding BLOB must SURVIVE untouched — the post-update dump still shows `0000003f000080be0000403f0000003e`. createdAt preserved, updatedAt pinned.

data.content: The Salon convenes at the stroke of midnight, lanterns lit.

ops.comment: TEXT-ONLY update on the NULL-embedding seed row (chunk c2): change content + chunkIndex. Embedding stays NULL, headingContext stays NULL. createdAt preserved, updatedAt pinned.

data.content: Prospero unfurls a grander map of the airship routes.

ops.comment: Delete the NULL-embedding `Carina` chunk created above.
